headline: LCS Programme: RM6.08B Spent, First Ship in Sea Trials, Original Schedule 7 Years Late
context: PAC's report on the Littoral Combat Ship programme found RM6.08B spent against an original contract value of RM9.13B (since revised to RM11.2B for 5 vessels). The first vessel began sea trials by 2026; none have been delivered to the Royal Malaysian Navy.

cards.big: RM6.08 billion spent. Zero ships delivered to the Royal Malaysian Navy. The first vessel is in sea trials.
cards.sub: LCS programme: contract signed 2011, first delivery due 2019. Original contract RM9.13B for 6 ships, revised to RM11.2B for 5 ships.

cards.big: PAC identified RM1.4B in cost overruns and allocations used for purposes other than the LCS works.
cards.sub: PAC also flagged 'pembayaran melebihi kerja sebenar' (payments exceeding actual work) under a progress-payment schedule weakly tied to milestones.

cards.big: MACC investigation papers were completed by August 2022. The former BNS managing director was charged with CBT involving RM21M.
cards.sub: BNS is government-linked through LTAT/Boustead/MOF ownership history. PAC findings included weak finances, failed due diligence, and a direct-negotiation award.

cards.big: PAC and audit findings carry serious defence and maritime-enforcement implications. Patrol relies on MMEA, RMN assets and Op Naga.
cards.sub: AG reporting cited 89 Chinese-vessel incursions from 2016-2019. The LCS shortfall is one input into a broader capability picture.

cards.big: The question is no longer whether RM6B bought ships. It is whether the revised contract, new payment terms and trial timeline will close the gap they opened.
Like paying a builder RM6B against a RM9B contract, finding partial hulls instead of a delivered fleet, and renegotiating with the same builder.
cards.sub: Independent oversight came from PAC, the National Audit Department, MACC and auditors, not from BNS itself.

cards.big: Accountability did happen: PAC reports, declassification, MACC charges, contract restructure. The unresolved test is delivery and whether the new terms hold.
